name: Daniel Miessler
why_notable: Created SecLists (2012) — the community-standard wordlist repository that became the canonical reference for pentest tooling. Before SecLists, wordlists were scattered across tools, distros, and personal pastebin dumps. SecLists centralized everything: passwords, usernames, fuzzing payloads, web paths, subdomains, discovery lists. Now at 58,000+ GitHub stars and referenced by virtually every pentest tool that ships with wordlists. Also writes the Unsupervised Learning newsletter on security and technology.
